title: Cyclea ochiaiana
taxonomy: Cyclea ochiaiana is classified in the family Annonaceae. This family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the custard apple or soursop family. The genus Cyclea is a group of flowering plants native to tropical Asia, and C. ochiaiana is one of the species within this genus. Further classification of C. ochiaiana would include its order (Magnoliales), class (Magnoliopsida), and division (Angiospermae). The plant's classification is an important part of its identification and understanding of its evolutionary history.

This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its numerous health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, respiratory infections, and digestive issues.
